//: Composite Firestore indexes, deployed per-context by
scripts/deploy-firestore-rules.js on every Netlify build -- same
mechanism, and the same reason, as firestore.rules: an index that only
exists because somebody once clicked a link in a Firestore error message
is an index that does not exist in the next project you deploy to.
Both indexes below are REQUIRED by netlify/functions/inspection-reports.js.
Without them Firestore returns 9 FAILED_PRECONDITION and the warranty
inspection pipeline breaks in the two places it can least afford to:
warranty_review_queue (status + createdAt)
list_review_queue() -- the REVIEW QUEUE itself. This is the safety net
for 'never silently attach a report to the wrong building': ambiguous
reports get staged here for Mark to assign by hand. Without the index
the queue write still succeeds but the LIST 500s -- so reports would be
parked in Firestore, invisible, and nobody would know to go get them.
Confirmed live on watkins-service-orders-dev, 2026-07-13.
warranty_reports (status + uploadedAt)
resolveSupersedes() -- called on EVERY filing, before the report doc is
written. Without the index this throws, so the attachment is neither
filed nor queued: it just lands in summary.errors and is retried
forever. Filing would have been broken outright.
